target.label: CCSR
target.title: CCSR DAG
target.subtitle: Lineage for the CCSR data mart rooted at its public output models.
target.graphSubtitle: Lineage for the CCSR data mart rooted at its public output models.

nodes.name: Condition
nodes.description: The core condition table contains information related to medical  conditions patients have, including problems, admitting diagnosis codes,  and billable diagnosis codes.

columns.name: condition_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each condition in the table.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each person in the dataset.

columns.name: member_id
columns.description: Identifier that links a patient to a particular insurance product or health plan. A patient can have more than one member_id because they can have more than one insurance product/plan.

columns.name: patient_id
columns.description: Identifier that links a patient to a particular clinical source system.

columns.name: encounter_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each encounter in the dataset.

columns.name: claim_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for a claim. Each claim represents a distinct healthcare service or set of services provided to a patient.

columns.name: recorded_date
columns.description: Date when the condition was recorded.

columns.name: onset_date
columns.description: Date when the condition first occurred.

columns.name: resolved_date
columns.description: Date when the condition was resolved.

columns.name: status
columns.description: Status of the record (e.g., condition, test, etc).

columns.name: condition_type
columns.description: The type of condition i.e. problem, admitting, or billing.

columns.name: source_code_type
columns.description: The type of code reported in the source system (e.g., ICD-10 code, NDC, lab, etc)

columns.name: source_code
columns.description: The code in the source system (e.g., the ICD-10 code, NDC, lab, etc)
columns.terminologyNote: **Note: this terminology set is too large to be loaded as a seed and is instead loaded from public cloud storage for supported adapters.

columns.name: source_description
columns.description: Description of the source code in the source system.

columns.name: normalized_code_type
columns.description: The normalized type of code.

columns.name: normalized_code
columns.description: The normalized code.
columns.terminologyNote: **Note: this terminology set is too large to be loaded as a seed and is instead loaded from public cloud storage for supported adapters.

columns.name: normalized_description
columns.description: Normalized description of the code.

columns.name: mapping_method
columns.description: mapping method used to populate the normalized codes and descriptions.  Can be manual (fields were populated in input layer), automatic (dictionary codes matching the source code were found and was automatically populated) or custom (populated by normalization engine)

columns.name: condition_rank
columns.description: The numerical ranking of the condition. For conditions derived from medical claims, condition_rank is set from the diagnosis code position on the claim, so diagnosis_code_1 becomes condition_rank = 1, diagnosis_code_2 becomes condition_rank = 2, and so on through diagnosis_code_25. For conditions sourced from the input layer condition table, this field is passed through without transformation from the mapped input value. A condition_rank of 1 indicates a primary diagnosis code, and any condition_rank greater than 1 indicates a secondary diagnosis code.

columns.name: present_on_admit_code
columns.description: The present_on_admit_code related to the condition.

columns.name: present_on_admit_description
columns.description: The description of the present_on_admit_code for the condition.

columns.name: data_source
columns.description: User-configured field that indicates the data source.

columns.name: tuva_last_run
columns.description: The last time the data was refreshed. Generated by `dbt_utils.pretty_time` as the local time of the `dbt run` environment. Timezone is configurable via the `tuva_last_run` var.

nodes.name: ccsr__dxccsr_v2023_1_body_systems
nodes.description:
A table containing descriptions of the top level categories used in the CCSR condition mapper.
This was manually extracted from the User Guide PDF included with the DXCCSR SAS program.

columns.name: body_system
columns.description: The body system associated with the CCSR parent category.

columns.name: ccsr_parent_category
columns.description: The parent category in the CCSR condition mapper.

columns.name: parent_category_description
columns.description: Description of the parent category in the CCSR condition mapper.

nodes.name: ccsr__dxccsr_v2023_1_cleaned_map
nodes.description:
A table containing the CSV mapping file included with the DXCCSR SAS program.
The original CSV was not natively seedable, and the file was transformed with the coded included in the README.
For each ICD-10-CM code, the table contains up to six CCSR category codes and a default CCSR category code.

columns.name: icd_10_cm_code
columns.description: ICD-10-CM code

columns.name: icd_10_cm_code_description
columns.description: Description of the ICD-10-CM code

columns.name: default_ccsr_category_ip
columns.description: Default CCSR category for inpatient settings

columns.name: default_ccsr_category_description_ip
columns.description: Description of the default CCSR category for inpatient settings

columns.name: default_ccsr_category_op
columns.description: Default CCSR category for outpatient settings

columns.name: default_ccsr_category_description_op
columns.description: Description of the default CCSR category for outpatient settings

columns.name: ccsr_category_1
columns.description: First CCSR category code

columns.name: ccsr_category_1_description
columns.description: Description of the first CCSR category code

columns.name: ccsr_category_2
columns.description: Second CCSR category code

columns.name: ccsr_category_2_description
columns.description: Description of the second CCSR category code

columns.name: ccsr_category_3
columns.description: Third CCSR category code

columns.name: ccsr_category_3_description
columns.description: Description of the third CCSR category code

columns.name: ccsr_category_4
columns.description: Fourth CCSR category code

columns.name: ccsr_category_4_description
columns.description: Description of the fourth CCSR category code

columns.name: ccsr_category_5
columns.description: Fifth CCSR category code

columns.name: ccsr_category_5_description
columns.description: Description of the fifth CCSR category code

columns.name: ccsr_category_6
columns.description: Sixth CCSR category code

columns.name: ccsr_category_6_description
columns.description: Description of the sixth CCSR category code

nodes.name: ccsr__prccsr_v2023_1_cleaned_map
nodes.description:
A table containing the CSV mapping file included with the PRCCSR SAS program.
The original CSV was not natively seedable, and the file was transformed with the coded included in the README.

columns.name: icd_10_pcs
columns.description: ICD-10-PCS code

columns.name: icd_10_pcs_description
columns.description: Description of the ICD-10-PCS code

columns.name: prccsr
columns.description: PRCCSR category code

columns.name: prccsr_description
columns.description: Description of the PRCCSR category code

columns.name: clinical_domain
columns.description: Clinical domain associated with the PRCCSR category

nodes.name: terminology__icd10_pcs_cms_ontology
nodes.description:
A table containing the broken out elements of the ontology associated with ICD-10-PCS codes. These codes are a positional ontology,
where each character in the code contains information about the procedure, depending on its position. CMS posts files containing these codes in XML format,
which can be tabularized using Python.

columns.name: icd10pcs_code
columns.description: ICD-10-PCS code

columns.name: section
columns.description: The "section" is associated with the first character of the ICD-10-PCS code.

columns.name: body_system
columns.description: Body system associated with the ICD-10-PCS code; corresponds to the second character of the code.

columns.name: operation
columns.description: Root operation associated with the ICD-10-PCS code; corresponds to the third character of the code

columns.name: body_part
columns.description: Body part associated with the ICD-10-PCS code; corresponds to the fourth character of the code

columns.name: approach
columns.description: Approach associated with the ICD-10-PCS code; corresponds to the fifth character of the code

columns.name: device
columns.description: Device associated with the ICD-10-PCS code; corresponds to the sixth character of the code

columns.name: qualifier
columns.description: Qualifier associated with the ICD-10-PCS code; corresponds to the seventh character of the code

nodes.name: ccsr__dx_vertical_pivot
nodes.description:
This is an intermediate model that pivots the DXCCSR's wide-format mapping CSV into a long format table. While the seed uses 6 columns to represent the 6 CCSR codes that may be present, this generates one row for each CCSR category per ICD code, so up to 6 rows per code.
This model includes details and descriptions of the CCSR parent category and body systems that are inherited by the final models.
This model may also be useful in looking up CCSR condition category,  body system, and parent category information.

columns.name: code
columns.description: The ICD-10-CM code

columns.name: code_description
columns.description: The description of the ICD-10-CM code included in the seed file.

columns.name: ccsr_category
columns.description: A CCSR category for the ICD-10-CM code. One ICD-10-CM code may have up 6 CCSR categories associated, ordinally ranked by the ccsr_category_rank. Will be null if a record has a `code` that isn't referenced in the CCSR seed file.

columns.name: ccsr_category_rank
columns.description: The ordinal rank of a given CCSR category mapped to an ICD-10-CM code.  The CCSR seed CSV has the order of categories assigned to each ICD-10-CM code.

columns.name: ccsr_category_description
columns.description: The human readable description of the `ccsr_category`.

columns.name: ccsr_parent_category
columns.description: The CCSR parent category - the three letter prefix for each alphanumeric category.

columns.name: is_ip_default_category
columns.description: For each ICD-10-CM code, this field will return true for the CCSR category that is the default value for the code if the underlyin record is an inpatient encounter.

columns.name: is_op_default_category
columns.description: For each ICD-10-CM code, this field will return true for the CCSR category that is the default value for the code if the underlyingrecord is an outpatient encounter.

columns.name: tuva_last_run
columns.description: The time at with the model was materialized. Generated by  `dbt_utils.pretty_time` as the local time of the `dbt run` environment.   Timezone is configurable via the `tuva_last_run` var.

nodes.name: ccsr__procedure_category_map
nodes.description: This is an intermediate model that renames some PRCCSR columns to align with other TUVA models as well as the `ccsr__dx_vertical_pivot` model.
nodes.sql:
{{ config(
     enabled = var('claims_enabled', var('clinical_enabled', False)) | as_bool
   )
}}

select
     icd_10_pcs as code
    , icd_10_pcs_description as code_description
    , prccsr as ccsr_category
    , substring(prccsr, 1, 3) as ccsr_parent_category
    , prccsr_description as ccsr_category_description
    , clinical_domain
    , ont.section as procedure_section
    , ont.operation
    , ont.approach
    , ont.device
    , ont.qualifier
    , cast('{{ var('tuva_last_run') }}' as {{ dbt.type_timestamp() }}) as tuva_last_run
from {{ ref('ccsr__prccsr_v2023_1_cleaned_map') }} as ccsr_map
left outer join {{ ref('terminology__icd10_pcs_cms_ontology') }} as ont
    on ccsr_map.icd_10_pcs = ont.icd10pcs_code

columns.name: code
columns.description: The ICD-10-PCS code

columns.name: code_description
columns.description: The description of the ICD-10-PCS code included in the seed file.

columns.name: ccsr_category
columns.description: The CCSR category to which the ICD-10 code is mapped.

columns.name: ccsr_category_description
columns.description: A description of the CCSR category.

columns.name: clinical_domain
columns.description: The clinical domain to which the CCSR code belongs. Note that the condition models don't have this column, but the `body_system` is a conceptually related column in the condition tables.

columns.name: ccsr_parent_category
columns.description: The CCSR parent category - the three letter prefix for each alphanumeric category.

columns.name: procedure_section
columns.description:
The first character in an ICD-10-PCS code, which determines the broad section
 where the code is found (e.g. Medical and Surgical)

columns.name: operation
columns.description: The objective of the procedure - corresponds to the third character in an ICD-10-PCS code

columns.name: approach
columns.description: The approach used to reach the site of the procedure - corresponds to the fifth character in an ICD-10-PCS code (e.g. Percutaneous Endoscopic)

columns.name: device
columns.description: If there is a device present after the procedure, specifies the specific device.  Corresponds to the sixth character in an ICD-10-PCS code

columns.name: qualifier
columns.description: Specifies additional part of the procedure if applicable - corresponds to the seventh character of an ICD-10-PCS code.

columns.name: tuva_last_run
columns.description: The time at with the model was materialized. Generated by  `dbt_utils.pretty_time` as the local time of the `dbt run` environment.   Timezone is configurable via the `tuva_last_run` var.

nodes.name: ccsr__stg_core__condition
nodes.sql:
{{ config(
     enabled = var('claims_enabled', var('clinical_enabled', False)) | as_bool
   )
}}

select
    encounter_id
    , claim_id
    , person_id
    , normalized_code
    , condition_rank
    , data_source
    , cast('{{ var('tuva_last_run') }}' as {{ dbt.type_timestamp() }}) as tuva_last_run
from {{ ref('core__condition') }}
where normalized_code_type = 'icd-10-cm'

nodes.name: ccsr__stg_core__procedure
ccsr__long_condition_category
nodes.description: This model contains a mapping of individual condition ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes to the CCSR's clinically meaningful diagnosis categories. Each row represents a mapping of an ICD-10 code to a CCSR category. As each ICD-10  code may be mapped to up to 6 categories, it's expected that this table will output as many or more rows than the TUVA condition model. The model is equivalent to the CCSR's Output Option 1 - Vertical File Output.
nodes.sql:
{{ config(
     enabled = var('claims_enabled', var('clinical_enabled', False)) | as_bool
   )
}}

with ccsr__dx_vertical_pivot as (

    select * from {{ ref('ccsr__dx_vertical_pivot') }}

)

, condition as (

    select * from {{ ref('ccsr__stg_core__condition') }}

)

, dxccsr_body_systems as (

    select * from {{ ref('ccsr__dxccsr_v2023_1_body_systems') }}

)

select
      condition.encounter_id
    , condition.claim_id
    , condition.person_id
    , condition.normalized_code
    , ccsr__dx_vertical_pivot.code_description
    , condition.condition_rank
    , ccsr__dx_vertical_pivot.ccsr_parent_category
    , dxccsr_body_systems.body_system
    , dxccsr_body_systems.parent_category_description
    , ccsr__dx_vertical_pivot.ccsr_category
    , ccsr__dx_vertical_pivot.ccsr_category_description
    , ccsr__dx_vertical_pivot.ccsr_category_rank
    , ccsr__dx_vertical_pivot.is_ip_default_category
    , ccsr__dx_vertical_pivot.is_op_default_category
    , condition.data_source
    , {{ var('dxccsr_version') }} as dxccsr_version
    , cast('{{ var('tuva_last_run') }}' as {{ dbt.type_timestamp() }}) as tuva_last_run
from condition
left outer join ccsr__dx_vertical_pivot
    on condition.normalized_code = ccsr__dx_vertical_pivot.code
left outer join dxccsr_body_systems on ccsr__dx_vertical_pivot.ccsr_parent_category = dxccsr_body_systems.ccsr_parent_category

columns.name: encounter_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each the encounter.

columns.name: claim_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each claim.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each patient.

columns.name: normalized_code
columns.description: The ICD-10-CM code for the diagnosis.
columns.terminologyNote: **Note: this terminology set is too large to be loaded as a seed and is instead loaded from public cloud storage for supported adapters.

columns.name: code_description
columns.description: The ICD-10-CM code description provided in the CCSR CSV seed file.

columns.name: condition_rank
columns.description: The numerical ranking of a diagnosis code in a claim.

columns.name: body_system
columns.description: The body system to which each parent category belongs. The 22 body systems generally follow the structure of the ICD-10-CM diagnosis chapters. Note that the procedure tables don't have this column, but a conceptually related field in procedure models is `clinical_domain`.

columns.name: ccsr_parent_category
columns.description: The parent category code for each CCSR category. In practice, this is the three letter prefix to the category code.

columns.name: parent_category_description
columns.description: A description of the parent category.

columns.name: ccsr_category
columns.description: A CCSR category for the ICD-10-CM code. One ICD-10-CM code may have up 6 CCSR categories associated, ordinally ranked by the ccsr_category_rank. Will be null if a record has a `code` that isn't referenced in the CCSR seed file.

columns.name: ccsr_category_description
columns.description: The description of the CCSR category as provided in the CCSR seed file.

columns.name: ccsr_category_rank
columns.description: The ordinal rank of a given CCSR category mapped to an ICD-10-CM code.  The CCSR seed CSV has the order of categories assigned to each ICD-10-CM code.

columns.name: is_ip_default_category
columns.description: For each ICD-10-CM code, this field will return true for the CCSR category that is the default value for the code if the underlying record is an inpatient encounter.

columns.name: is_op_default_category
columns.description: For each ICD-10-CM code, this field will return true for the CCSR category that is the default value for the code if the underlying record is an outpatient encounter.

columns.name: dxccsr_version
columns.description: The version number of the CCSR program from which the dbt model was derived.

columns.name: tuva_last_run
columns.description: The time at with the model was materialized. Generated by  `dbt_utils.pretty_time` as the local time of the `dbt run` environment.   Timezone is configurable via the `tuva_last_run` var.

nodes.name: ccsr__long_procedure_category
nodes.description: This model contains a mapping of individual condition ICD-10-PCS procedure codes to the CCSR's clinically meaningful procedure categories. Each row represents a mapping of an ICD-10 code to a CCSR category. The model is equivalent to the CCSR's Output Option 1 - Vertical File Output.
nodes.sql:
{{ config(
     enabled = var('claims_enabled', var('clinical_enabled', False)) | as_bool
   )
}}

with procedures as (

    select * from {{ ref('ccsr__stg_core__procedure') }}

)

, ccsr__procedure_category_map as (

    select * from {{ ref ('ccsr__procedure_category_map') }}

)

select distinct
      procedures.encounter_id
    , procedures.claim_id
    , procedures.person_id
    , procedures.normalized_code
    , ccsr__procedure_category_map.code_description
    , ccsr__procedure_category_map.ccsr_parent_category
    , ccsr__procedure_category_map.ccsr_category
    , ccsr__procedure_category_map.ccsr_category_description
    , ccsr__procedure_category_map.clinical_domain
    , ccsr__procedure_category_map.procedure_section
    , ccsr__procedure_category_map.operation
    , ccsr__procedure_category_map.approach
    , ccsr__procedure_category_map.device
    , ccsr__procedure_category_map.qualifier
    , procedures.data_source
    , {{ var('prccsr_version') }} as prccsr_version
    , cast('{{ var('tuva_last_run') }}' as {{ dbt.type_timestamp() }}) as tuva_last_run
from procedures
inner join ccsr__procedure_category_map
    on procedures.normalized_code = ccsr__procedure_category_map.code

columns.name: encounter_id
columns.description: The encounter_id for the encounter where this procedure was performed.

columns.name: claim_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each claim.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each patient.

columns.name: normalized_code
columns.description: The ICD-10-PCS code for the procedure.
columns.terminologyNote: **Note: this terminology set is too large to be loaded as a seed and is instead loaded from public cloud storage for supported adapters.

columns.name: code_description
columns.description: The ICD-10-PCS code description provided in the CCSR CSV seed file.

columns.name: ccsr_parent_category
columns.description: The parent category code for each CCSR category. In practice, this is the three letter prefix to the category code.

columns.name: clinical_domain
columns.description: The ICD-10-PCS Clinical Domain that the CCSR parent category belongs to. Note that the condition models don't have this column, but the  `body_system` is a conceptually related column in the condition tables.

columns.name: ccsr_category_description
columns.description: A description of the clinical domain.

columns.name: ccsr_category
columns.description: The CCSR category mapped to the ICD-10-PCS code.

columns.name: prccsr_version
columns.description: The version number of the CCSR program from which the dbt model was derived.

columns.name: tuva_last_run
columns.description: The time at with the model was materialized. Generated by  `dbt_utils.pretty_time` as the local time of the `dbt run` environment.   Timezone is configurable via the `tuva_last_run` var.

nodes.name: ccsr__procedure_summary
nodes.description: This table stores information on procedure volume patterns across CCSR categories.
nodes.sql:
{{ config(
     enabled = var('claims_enabled', var('clinical_enabled', False)) | as_bool
   )
}}

with procedure_base as (
    select
        encounter_id
        , claim_id
        , normalized_code
        , code_description
        , ccsr_parent_category
        , ccsr_category
        , ccsr_category_description
        , clinical_domain
        , operation
        , approach
        , count(claim_id) over
            (partition by ccsr_category, operation) as n_total_occurrences
    from {{ ref('ccsr__long_procedure_category') }}
    -- include only records that map to a CCSR procedure category
    where ccsr_category is not null
)

, procedures_aggregated as (
    select
        ccsr_category
        , ccsr_category_description
        , operation
        , approach
        , count(claim_id) as n_occurrences_with_approach
        , n_total_occurrences
        , count(claim_id) / n_total_occurrences * 100 as approach_rate
    from procedure_base
    group by
        ccsr_category
        , ccsr_category_description
        , operation
        , approach
        , n_total_occurrences
)

select
    *
    , cast('{{ var('tuva_last_run') }}' as {{ dbt.type_timestamp() }}) as tuva_last_run
from procedures_aggregated

columns.name: ccsr_category
columns.description: The CCSR category mapped to the ICD-10-PCS code associated with the procedure

columns.name: ccsr_category_description
columns.description: A description of the clinical domain.

columns.name: operation
columns.description: The objective of the procedure performed

columns.name: approach
columns.description: The way the surgical site was reached

columns.name: n_occurrences_with_approach
columns.description: within a CCSR category, the number of times a specific approach occurred for a given operation.

columns.name: n_total_occurrences
columns.description: for a given CCSR category, the number of times specific operation occurred

columns.name: approach_rate
columns.description: Of all occurrences of an operation within a CCSR category, the rate at whiich a specific approach occurred.

nodes.name: ccsr__singular_condition_category
nodes.description: This model contains only the CCSR's default category assignment for the ICD-10 code, and only for the first-listed ICD-10 code  (`diagnosis_code = 1`).
nodes.sql:
{{ config(
     enabled = var('claims_enabled', var('clinical_enabled', False)) | as_bool
   )
}}

select
      encounter_id
    , claim_id
    , person_id
    , normalized_code
    , code_description
    , ccsr_category
    , ccsr_category_description
    , ccsr_parent_category
    , parent_category_description
    , body_system
    , data_source
    , {{ var('dxccsr_version') }} as dxccsr_version
    , cast('{{ var('tuva_last_run') }}' as {{ dbt.type_timestamp() }}) as tuva_last_run
from {{ ref('ccsr__long_condition_category') }}
where is_{{ var('record_type', 'ip') }}_default_category = 1
and condition_rank = 1

columns.name: encounter_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for an encounter.

columns.name: claim_id
columns.description: Unique claim_id for each claim.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each patient.

columns.name: normalized_code
columns.description: The ICD-10-CM code for the diagnosis.
columns.terminologyNote: **Note: this terminology set is too large to be loaded as a seed and is instead loaded from public cloud storage for supported adapters.

columns.name: code_description
columns.description: The ICD-10-CM code description provided in the CCSR CSV seed file.

columns.name: body_system
columns.description: The body system to which each parent category belongs. The 22 body systems generally follow the structure of the ICD-10-CM diagnosis chapters. Note that the procedure tables don't have this column, but a conceptually related field in procedure models is `clinical_domain`.

columns.name: ccsr_parent_category
columns.description: The parent category code for each CCSR category. In practice, this is the three letter prefix to the category code.

columns.name: parent_category_description
columns.description: A description of the parent category.

columns.name: ccsr_category
columns.description: A CCSR category for the ICD-10-CM code. One ICD-10-CM code may have up 6 CCSR categories associated, ordinally ranked by the ccsr_category_rank. Will be null if a record has a `code` that isn't referenced in the CCSR seed file.

columns.name: ccsr_category_description
columns.description: The human readable description of the `ccsr_category`.

columns.name: dxccsr_version
columns.description: The version number of the CCSR program from which the dbt model was derived.

columns.name: tuva_last_run
columns.description: The time at with the model was materialized. Generated by  `dbt_utils.pretty_time` as the local time of the `dbt run` environment.   Timezone is configurable via the `tuva_last_run` var.
